titel: Reduced alcohol tax for alcoholic beverages from independent small producers.
html:
On July 1, 2025, Swedish regulations were introduced regarding a reduced tax rate for beer from independent microbreweries. The proposal suggests a reduced alcohol tax for other alcoholic beverages from independent small producers, under certain conditions.

It is proposed that the reduction should apply to alcoholic beverages produced by producers whose annual production does not exceed 100,000 liters of wine, 1.5 million liters of other fermented beverages, 25,000 liters of intermediate products, or, in the case of ethyl alcohol, 1,000 liters of pure alcohol. The producers who are eligible for the reduced tax rate are divided into five different categories based on annual production. Alcoholic beverages from producers in the category with the lowest production receive a tax reduction of 50 percent. The reduction then decreases gradually by 10 percentage points per category.

The changes are proposed to come into effect on July 1, 2026.

Proposed Parliamentary Decision

Prop. 2025/26:183

Government's proposal:

  1. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the law (2022:156) on alcohol tax.
  2. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the law on tax procedures (2011:1244).

3.

  1. whose annual production of the

relevant alcoholic beverage does not exceed the following levels: a) beer: 3,000,000 liters per year, b) wine: 100,000 liters per year, c) other fermented beverages: 1,500,000 liters per year,

Prop. 2025/26:183 2
tldr: Small producers of alcoholic beverages should pay less tax. This is to help smaller breweries and wineries operate in Sweden.
lattlast:
The government has proposed a plan to reduce the tax on alcohol for small producers. This means that those who produce smaller quantities of beer, wine, and other beverages will not have to pay as much tax as the large corporations. The proposal aims to support independent microbreweries and other small-scale manufacturers.

The amount of the tax reduction depends on the amount of beverage the company produces each year. The smallest producers will receive the largest reduction, with the tax decreasing by 50 percent. The more beverages a company produces, the smaller the tax reduction will be. There are different limits on the amount of beer, wine, and other beverages that can be produced for a company to be considered a small producer.

To qualify for the lower tax rate, there are limits on the number of liters of beverages that can be produced per year. For example, a wine producer can produce a maximum of 100,000 liters of wine to be eligible for the rules. The limit for beer is higher, up to 3 million liters per year. There are also regulations for other types of beverages and pure alcohol.

If the parliament votes in favor of the proposal, the new rules will come into effect on July 1, 2026. The proposal also includes changes to the laws governing how taxes are paid to the state.
